data.title: Director – Infrastructure Operations
data.jd_text: Established in 2004, Dubai Holding is a global investment company with investments in more than 34 countries and a combined workforce of nearly 45,000 individuals. In line with the vision of Dubai’s leadership and economic diversification strategy, Dubai Holding companies have nurtured sectors, irrevocably transforming Dubai’s socio-economic landscape and positioning Dubai as a diversified, globally integrated economy. Dubai Holding is committed to the diversification of Dubai’s non-oil economy. Our portfolio, valued at over AED 284+ billion, spans 10 sectors, including real estate, hospitality, leisure & entertainment, ICT, design, education, media, retail, manufacturing & logistics, and science. For the Good of Tomorrow Dubai Holding is looking to hire a Director - Infrastructure Operations reporting to the Vice President - Cloud, Infrastructure and Workplace Services. This is a senior leadership role accountable for the design, delivery, and continuous evolution of Dubai Holding's infrastructure services portfolio, spanning compute, storage, data centre, network, and Microsoft 365 environments. The role holds end-to-end ownership of infrastructure operations across the organisation, orchestrating the combined capabilities of internal teams, strategic third-party partners, and emerging technologies to deliver resilient, secure, and high-performing services at scale. A core contributor to the group-wide infrastructure strategy and technology roadmap, the Director plays an integral role in shaping and executing the direction of Dubai Holding's infrastructure function — ensuring all solutions and initiatives are grounded in sound architectural principles, aligned to security and audit requirements, and structured to deliver tangible business value. The Director is a change agent who thrives in a dynamic, fast-paced environment. The role brings together strategic vision and operational rigour to drive technology modernisation, elevate service quality, enhance customer satisfaction, and achieve sustainable operational efficiency across the organisation. If you are ready to embark on a dynamic journey where your talents are valued, and your contributions make a real impact, we would like to hear from you. Key Responsibilities: Define and own the organisation's infrastructure architecture strategy, ensuring solutions are scalable, resilient, and secure. Partner with cross-functional teams and business verticals to design and deliver infrastructure that meets both current demands and future growth requirements. Take executive ownership of the availability, stability, recoverability, and performance of all infrastructure services across the organisation, ensuring they remain cost-optimised, fit for purpose, and aligned with agreed service levels. We take concepts from zero to launch, and when a venture proves its potential, we back it with seed funding and hands on support to help it scale. This venture is one of those bets an open banking consumer micro-lending platform in Saudi Arabia that uses real-time financial data and a smart decision engine to offer fast, personalized micro-loans to consumers who are priced out or passed over by traditional lenders. We are looking for a Chief Operations Officer to lead Finance, Accounting, HR, Collections, and Customer Care and to build the operational backbone that takes this venture from early stage to scale. data.title: Regional Director of Operations - KSA
data.jd_text: With over 15+ hotels in the Kingdom, Millennium is growing fast and recruiting a Regional Director of Operations. This newly created role is pivotal in evaluating and aligning with the short and long-term goals of the existing and new hotels in the Kingdom. Operating under the direct supervision of the Managing Director, this role acts as a trusted, senior-level operational resource, providing hands-on support to property-level teams to resolve operational challenges, elevate service standards, and drive commercial outcomes. The successful candidate will have around 15-18 years hospitality experience, be a seasoned leader with strong multi-property operational expertise, capable of mobilizing quickly to address urgent issues, stabilize performance, and support projects. He/she will also act as a corporate-level ambassador for operational excellence, brand consistency, and pre-opening readiness. Arabic speaker a plus, and previous P&L management as well as KSA experience a must. Key Job Responsibilities include: Provide operational support and performance oversight as well as ensure standards implementation and compliance Provide on-ground support to managed properties experiencing operational performance gaps or transitional challenges Serve as the point-of-contact for operational interventions and temporary leadership reinforcement at property level Implement brand standards, SOPs, and service recovery protocols where needed Collaborate with hotel General Managers to align operational strategies with Group objectives Monitor KPIs, guest satisfaction scores, and financial metrics to diagnose operational bottlenecks and propose solutions Only shortlisted applicants will be notified. Thank you for your interest!

data.title: Managing Director KSA (General Manager)
data.jd_text: About the Role: We are a fast-growing wealth-tech company seeking an experienced Managing Director in Riyadh with proven fintech/wealth-tech experience in the KSA. This is a CMA-registrable senior executive role (equivalent to CEO/General Manager under CMIR Articles 19-20), requiring CMA approval and fit and proper status. You will serve as the company’s primary representative in the Kingdom, acting as the main point of contact with the CMA and overseeing day-to-day operations. You will report to the board of directors, collaborate closely with the Compliance Officer, MLRO, operations teams, and external advisors, and ensure full alignment with CMA regulatory requirements, client protection principles, and legal compliance for arranging and advising activities. Key Responsibilities Strategy & Vision : Develop and execute the market entry plan, including strategic alignment with CMA-authorized activities (arranging introductions and advising on investments) and long-term business objectives. Operations Management & Licensing : Lead company setup, obtain all necessary CMA licenses and approvals (including nomination of registered persons), establish head office in the Kingdom, and ensure efficient, compliant operations with robust systems and controls. Financial Oversight : Oversee financial planning, budgeting, and reporting for the KSA entity, ensuring accuracy, transparency, and compliance with CMA disclosure standards. Leadership & Team Management : Lead hiring, onboarding, and development of the local team; foster company culture; implement training programs on CMA regulations, ethics, and compliance. Stakeholder Relations : Build and maintain relationships with partners, suppliers, clients, investors, and local regulators (primarily CMA). Compliance & Governance : Ensure adherence to CMA regulations, including client classification, suitability assessments for advice, risk disclosures, conflicts of interest management, fair treatment, and fiduciary duties. Appoint, resource, and supervise the Compliance Officer and MLRO (or oversee outsourced roles); promote ethical standards and a compliance culture. Risk Management & Internal Controls : Oversee establishment and regular review of risk management frameworks, AML/CTF procedures, business continuity plans, and internal controls; ensure timely CMA notifications (e.g., material changes, breaches). Regulatory Engagement : Act as primary liaison with the CMA, including supporting audits, inquiries, license renewals/variations, and submissions.  Client Protection & Licensed Activities : Oversee processes for KYC, client due diligence, suitability reports (where required), and use of technology in advice delivery (with CMA approval if needed). Performance & Reporting : Provide regular reports to the board on operations, compliance, risks, and regulatory matters; participate in board meetings and committee work as required. data.title: Head of Information Technology
data.jd_text: We are partnering with a well-established, diversified UAE-based group to appoint an experienced IT Director to lead and oversee enterprise-wide technology strategy and operations. This role offers the opportunity to drive digital transformation initiatives and shape the long-term technology vision of a multi-entity organization. Role Overview The IT Director will be responsible for defining and executing the organization’s IT strategy while ensuring secure, efficient, and scalable technology operations across multiple business divisions. The role requires a strategic leader capable of aligning technology with business objectives while maintaining operational excellence. Key Responsibilities • Develop and implement the overall IT strategy aligned with organizational goals • Lead IT operations including infrastructure, networks, enterprise applications, and cybersecurity • Drive digital transformation and process automation initiatives • Establish IT governance, policies, risk management, and compliance frameworks • Manage IT budgets, vendor relationships, and technology procurement • Ensure business continuity, data protection, and information security standards • Build, mentor, and lead high-performing IT teams across business units • Partner with senior leadership to enable innovation and technology-driven growth Requirements • Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or related field (Master’s preferred) • 12+ years of IT experience, including senior leadership roles • Proven experience within large or multi-entity organizations • Strong expertise in IT strategy, enterprise systems, and digital transformation • Experience managing large-scale IT environments and cross-functional teams • Strong stakeholder management and leadership capabilities • UAE or regional experience preferred Due to confidentiality, company details will be shared with shortlisted candidates.
